title: oenanthe biebersteinii var. vulgaris
intro: Oenanthe biebersteinii var. vulgaris, commonly known as water celery or water parsley, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the carrot family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting kidney function. In herbal preparations, water celery is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
